# Regression test for a real, reproduced bug: starting `breeze operator notify`
# fresh used to replay every PRE-EXISTING pending approval/failure as a desktop
# notification burst, since its seen-maps started empty and treated "already there"
# the same as "just happened." A fake `notify-send` on PATH records its calls
# instead of touching the real desktop, so this is hermetic.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state
env PATH=$WORK/bin:$PATH
chmod 0755 bin/notify-send

exec breeze identity register ci
cp stdout ci.token
exec breeze apply -f pipeline.hcl --as ci --token-file ci.token

# A failure that happens BEFORE the watcher ever starts — this is the "already in
# history at connect time" case that must NOT be notified about.
! exec breeze stage start release build stalecommit --as ci
stdout 'build: failed'

exec breeze operator notify &notify&
exec sleep 0.5
! exists notify-calls.log

# A failure that happens AFTER the watcher is already running — this one is
# genuinely new and must be notified.
! exec breeze stage start release build freshcommit --as ci
stdout 'build: failed'
exec sleep 0.5

exists notify-calls.log
! grep 'stalecommit' notify-calls.log
grep 'freshcommit' notify-calls.log

kill
exec breeze stop

-- bin/notify-send --
#!/bin/sh
echo "$@" >> notify-calls.log

-- pipeline.hcl --
pipeline "release" {
  stage "build" {
    type    = "command"
    timeout = "10s"
    command = ["/bin/false"]
  }
}
